response.title: 90-Day Throughput Trend
response.def1Desc: Calculated as a unique count of all shipments active during the analysis period. Each shipment is counted once based on its presence within the period, excluding any cumulative calculation of daily dwell time.
response.def2Desc: Average number of active shipments per day during the selected period.
response.def3Desc: Total shipments departed (Gate-out) on the specific date.
response.trendSummary: Over the past 90 days, throughput stabilized at a daily average of approximately 180 units. However, a recovery trend is currently observed following temporary fluctuations (surge and dip) during the year-end and New Year period.
response.bullet3: Recovery: Rapid return to standard levels post-holiday.
response.period1Desc: Year-end outbound surge increased daily departures. Extended lead times contributed to overall rise in in-transit volume.
response.period2Desc: Holiday impact reduced new departures, leading to gradual decline in total in-transit volume.
response.period3Desc: Departures normalized post-holiday. In-transit throughput showing steady recovery to average levels.

prompt2Response.title: Top 3 High-Risk Shipments (Past Year)
prompt2Response.description: Analysis of the past year's transit history indicates that quality risks were primarily driven by cumulative environmental deviations (temperature, humidity, shock) across the entire journey, rather than issues in specific single segments. Notably, all Top 3 cases exhibited recurring excursion patterns throughout the route, rather than isolated, one-time events.
prompt2Response.overviewCriteria: Risk Criteria: Excursions (Temp/Humidity/Shock/Light), Recurring patterns, Critical events.
prompt2Response.overviewPerspective: Focus: Cumulative risk & Recurrence probability (vs. Isolated events).
prompt2Response.top3Title: Top 3 Shipments: Highest Quality Risk Probability
prompt2Response.insightBullet1: Top 3 cases show cumulative risks across the entire route (vs. isolated events). Recurring patterns detected at specific transfer points.
prompt2Response.insightBullet2: Despite varying risk types, all share a common pattern: Cumulative impact from intermittent environmental fluctuations (Temp/Shock).
prompt2Response.insightBullet3: Risks are attributed to overall transit consistency and handover management, rather than isolated point failures.
prompt2Response.insightBullet4: AI Agent Selection: Shipments with highest relative quality risk probability based on 1-year history.
prompt2Response.mainIssueDesc:
Quality risks concentrated in transshipment and ocean phases of multimodal transport.

Inland/Storage segments stable. Risk drivers: Recurring temp/shock events on Busan–Seattle (Ocean/Terminal) leg.
prompt2Response.analysisDesc:
Ocean segment showed cumulative temp. excursions & spikes. Frequent shocks at Seattle Terminal caused 'Critical' risk levels. Root cause: Process control limitations in specific segments.

Zero excursions in origin/destination legs. Disparity indicates inconsistent management levels across route segments.
prompt2Response.analysisDesc2: Currently, response time for quality risk events averages 2-4 hours. Verification of SLA-based alert confirmation and response processes with local logistics centers and partners is recommended.

prompt3Response.title: Top 3 Bio-SLA Non-Compliance Shipments (Past Year)
prompt3Response.statusDesc1: Scope: All bio cold chain shipments (Past Year)
prompt3Response.statusDesc2: SLA Criteria: Temperature compliance, Critical events
prompt3Response.insightBullet1: Yearly Bio-SLA Analysis: Majority of shipments met Temp/Humidity standards. Exceptions show recurring excursions or response delays.
prompt3Response.insightBullet2: SLA non-compliance concentrated in specific segments (Nighttime Inland Transit & Unloading), rather than distributed across the entire route.
prompt3Response.insightBullet3: High-risk cases driven by operational process limitations, rather than external environmental conditions.
prompt3Response.insightBullet4: AI-generated summary based on 1-year SLA compliance & risk history. Select specific shipments for detailed segment analysis.
prompt3Response.mainIssueDesc1: Temperature excursion detected during U.S. Inland Transit (Early Morning). Prolonged response time indicates monitoring blind spots. Operational protocol improvement required.
prompt3Response.mainIssueDesc2: Other segments remained stable with zero SLA violations. Significant management disparity observed between Inland Transit vs. other segments.
prompt3Response.analysisDesc: ICN–LAX Air Segment: Consistently high SLA compliance across all similar bio-shipments. Demonstrates structurally sound operations and robust response systems.
prompt3Response.improvementDesc: Adopting the stable ICN–LAX operational framework as a benchmark to optimize U.S. inland transit is recommended for enhanced efficiency and quality.
prompt3Response.graph1Alt: U.S. inland transit temperature trend chart
prompt3Response.graph2Alt: Risk score by transit phase chart
prompt3Response.comparisonTitle: Risk Comparison: ICN–LAX (Air) vs. Major Routes
prompt3Response.comparisonDesc: Benchmarking ICN–LAX risk score against average risk of major outbound routes from Korea.
prompt3Response.comparisonNote: Lower scores indicate more stable performance.

alert.preparingResponse: Confirming temperature excursion on Pallet 10 and preparing appropriate response measures.
alert.title:
Temperature excursion detected on Pallet 10.
High risk of exceeding 46.4°F threshold in 15 mins.
alert.description: Current Temp: 41.4°F (+2.2°F above target). Rapid rise detected in last 15 mins. Immediate cooling adjustment required.
alert.forecastDesc: AI Forecast: Temperature likely to exceed 46.4°F limit in 15 mins. Primary Cause: Fan motor anomaly (87% match). Risk: Potential cargo loss.
alert.rcaDesc: Analysis of 67 historical shipments & 112 equipment patterns indicates 87% match with cooling fan failure.
alert.recommendedDesc: Increase cooling output and reduce speed in Pallet 10 zone to mitigate temp. excursion. Execute automated response?

action.title: Executing temperature excursion response protocol.
action.description: AI Agent assumed control: Adjusting cooling system.

tasks.alertSub2: App push sent to Mike Park (Transport Manager)
tasks.alertSub3: Sending Email to Jane Kim (Quality Manager)
tasks.targetSpeed: Target speed reached & max cooling activated

completion.title: Recovered to normal range (40.6°F) in 5 mins 15 secs.
completion.description: Situation resolved as of 7:01:30 AM. Temperature stabilized through max cooling system operation and vehicle speed reduction. Cargo is currently in normal condition. Detailed action records have been automatically saved to the report below.
completion.resultSummaryDesc: Analysis of similar past cases shows no impact on pharmaceutical quality when resolved within 5 mins 15 secs. Temperature history is automatically recorded for quality assurance documentation.
completion.followUpActionsDesc: Analysis of similar past cases shows no impact on pharmaceutical quality when resolved within 5 mins 15 secs. Temperature history is automatically recorded for quality assurance documentation.

autoTasks.eventLogSub: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliant, encrypted storage complete
autoTasks.tempHistorySub: SET_SPEED_LIMIT = 31mph command packet sent to Vehicle Control Unit (ECU)

scheduledTasks.coolingInspection: Cooling system inspection scheduled upon NYC arrival
scheduledTasks.coolingInspectionSub: NYC logistics center maintenance team assigned

fileUpload.caution2: Do not alter column order or format.

heatExposure.title: Accumulated heat exposure during sea transit: High risk to semiconductor quality.
heatExposure.description:
Repeated temperatures > 77.0℉ in sea transit (Peak:
119.3℉). Immediate cooling inspection and quality.

shockUnloading.title: Recurring shocks detected during unloading at Seattle terminal; high risk to cargo.
shockUnloading.description: 281 shock alerts triggered (Peak: 9.9G). Urgent review of unloading SOPs and impact mitigation.

temperatureFluctuation.title: Rapid temperature fluctuations detected in multimodal transit; thermal shock risk.
temperatureFluctuation.description: Temperature variance > 50°F observed on Busan–Seattle leg. Reinforce insulation and control temp.

minorShocks.title: Minor shocks detected during rail transit; continuous monitoring recommended.
minorShocks.description: 5 minor shocks on Chicago rail leg. Historical data implies negligible quality impact; product integrity.

temperature119.title: Temperature recorded at 119.3℉
temperature119.description:
Temperature is 42.3°F above 77.0°F set point; extreme risk to
semiconductor quality. Immediate cooling
measures required.

shock99G.title: 9.9G shock detected
shock99G.description:
Detected shock exceeds 2G limit by 7.9G. Immediate emergency
inspection required to verify cargo integrity.

prolongedTemp.title: Prolonged temperature exposure > 77.0°F
prolongedTemp.description:
Temperature remained > 77.0°F for 38.6% of transit. Prolonged
heat exposure poses high risk of altering semiconductor
electrical properties.

shocksSeattle.title: 261 shocks detected at Seattle Terminal
shocksSeattle.description: 261 shocks exceeding 2G limit recorded. High risk of semiconductor package damage due to recurring impact.

humidity80.title: Humidity recorded at 80%
humidity80.description: Humidity exceeded 60% limit by 20%. Temporary high-humidity detected; continuous monitoring required.

(Above Limit). {{count}} excursions indicate critical instability.

Humidity stable ({{minRange}}–{{maxRange}}%). Moisture ingress prevented. Caution: Potential
condensation risk during unloading due to rapid temp/humidity shifts. Inspect
cargo upon arrival.
descriptions.shock:
Shock threshold ({{maxRange}}{{unit}}) exceeded during Seattle unloading. Risk of physical damage.
Detailed inspection of product exterior & packaging integrity is strongly
recommended.
descriptions.light:
Single light detection confirmed during Daejeon gate-out (Standard Process).
No unauthorized openings detected thereafter. Cargo security maintained throughout transit.

All readings are within the configured range.
